skill.name: perigon-angular
skill.description: Guides Angular 21+ frontend work for the Perigon WebApp using standalone components, Angular Material, signals, services, i18n, and project conventions.
skill.summary: Apply Perigon Angular WebApp conventions for components, routing, services, state, styles, i18n, and tooling.
skill.author: AterDev

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one high weak-cryptography pattern and three low system-reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found only Angular documentation and project conventions, with no executable code, prompt injection, credential access, network access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is front matter text describing Angular 21+ conventions. No cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only a skill description. There is no code or crypto-related instruction to execute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line gives Angular component and styling conventions. It does not ask the agent to inspect host systems, collect environment details, or enumerate files outside the project context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is a framework style guideline for Angular and Material. No reconnaissance behavior or command execution is described.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line recommends Angular signals and signal forms. It does not instruct system probing, host discovery, credential collection, or hidden file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is limited to Angular state and form preferences. It contains no operational steps that affect system security.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line mentions pnpm, builds, tests, ESLint, and TypeScript defaults as project tooling guidance. It does not direct automated scanning or unauthorized system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is ordinary development workflow guidance. The instruction to avoid builds and tests unless requested reduces unintended command execution.

content.user_title: Build Angular Apps with Perigon Conventions
content.value_statement: Angular work can drift when routing, services, styles, i18n, and state patterns are not consistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to follow the Perigon WebApp conventions.
actual_capabilities: Guides Angular 21+ work toward standalone components instead of NgModules.
Identifies expected Perigon WebApp paths for routes, layout, pages, shared UI, pipes, and guards.
Recommends Angular Material, local SCSS placement, and established theme files.
Encourages signals and signal forms for state and form work.
Documents service, API client, interceptor, proxy, environment, i18n, auth, and paginator conventions.
Notes pnpm usage and existing ESLint and TypeScript defaults.
limitations: It is specific to the Perigon WebApp Angular project layout.
It provides conventions, not executable automation or generated project scaffolding.
It does not replace review of existing application code before editing.
It discourages builds and tests unless the user requests them.

use_cases.title: Add a WebApp page
use_cases.description: Use the expected pages, routes, layout, styles, and Angular Material patterns when creating a new frontend page.

use_cases.title: Update Angular state and forms
use_cases.description: Prefer signals and signal forms when changing component state, while avoiding older two-way binding where signals fit.

use_cases.title: Align services and localization
use_cases.description: Keep API clients, environment endpoints, interceptors, translation keys, and i18n files consistent with the project structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review a simple component change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular component change for Perigon WebApp conventions. Check standalone usage, Material imports, styles, and signal-friendly state.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a new page
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a new Perigon WebApp page. Include route placement, page directory, layout reuse, shared components, styles, and i18n keys.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor forms to signals
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Angular form toward signals and signal forms. Preserve behavior, Material controls, validation, and local component styles.

prompt_templates.title: Audit API integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Perigon Angular API integration. Check service placement, generated types, base service use, interceptor compatibility, environment config, and proxy assumptions.

output_examples.input: Create a new admin dashboard page for the WebApp.
output: Suggested page placement under the Perigon pages directory.
Route and layout notes aligned with the app routing structure.
Material component and SCSS placement guidance.

output_examples.input: Review this component that uses two-way binding.
output: Assessment of whether signals fit the state flow.
Recommended standalone component and Material import adjustments.
Notes about keeping styles beside the component.
Testing or build command guidance only if requested.

output_examples.input: Add a service that calls a backend endpoint.
output: Recommended service location in the application services area.
Endpoint configuration notes for environment files and proxy usage.
Reminder to keep the customer HTTP interceptor active.

best_practices: Read the nearby Angular files before applying the conventions.
Keep component styles beside their component SCSS files.
Use existing i18n key files and translation JSON files for user-facing strings.
anti_patterns: Adding NgModules for new feature work.
Bypassing existing services, interceptors, or environment configuration.
Running builds or tests automatically when the user did not request them.

faq.question: What type of projects does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Angular 21+ frontend work in the Perigon WebApp structure.

faq.question: Does it generate a full Angular application?
faq.answer: No. It guides changes inside an existing Perigon WebApp project.

faq.question: Which Angular patterns does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers standalone components, Angular Material, signals, signal forms, and local SCSS files.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive automation?
faq.answer: No. The skill file contains documentation only and no executable scripts.

faq.question: How should API work be handled?
faq.answer: Use the established services area, generated types, base service patterns, interceptors, proxy config, and environment files.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
